question: The concept of 'project management lifecycle'
options: Hierarchically decomposes project work into manageable components
Project tasks, durations and their timeline in a bar chart format
Initiation, planning, execution, monitoring and closing
Tasks that cannot begin until other tasks are completed
answer: Initiation, planning, execution, monitoring and closing

question: A 'Gantt chart' shows
options: The amount of time a task can be delayed without affecting the project end date
Initiation, planning, execution, monitoring and closing
Planning, monitoring and controlling project costs
Project tasks, durations and their timeline in a bar chart format
answer: Project tasks, durations and their timeline in a bar chart format

question: The concept of 'project scope'
options: Hierarchically decomposes project work into manageable components
The amount of time a task can be delayed without affecting the project end date
Planning, monitoring and controlling project costs
The defined boundaries of what a project will and will not deliver
answer: The defined boundaries of what a project will and will not deliver

question: 'Project stakeholders' include
options: Iterative approach delivering value in short cycles with continuous feedback
Completing all activities, delivering outputs and documenting lessons learned
All individuals and groups with an interest in or affected by the project
Initiation, planning, execution, monitoring and closing
answer: All individuals and groups with an interest in or affected by the project

question: The concept of 'risk register'
options: A document identifying potential project risks and mitigation strategies
The defined boundaries of what a project will and will not deliver
All individuals and groups with an interest in or affected by the project
Iterative approach delivering value in short cycles with continuous feedback
answer: A document identifying potential project risks and mitigation strategies

question: 'Work breakdown structure' (WBS)
options: Hierarchically decomposes project work into manageable components
Iterative approach delivering value in short cycles with continuous feedback
The defined boundaries of what a project will and will not deliver
A document identifying potential project risks and mitigation strategies
answer: Hierarchically decomposes project work into manageable components

question: The concept of 'project milestones'
options: Completing all activities, delivering outputs and documenting lessons learned
Tasks that cannot begin until other tasks are completed
All individuals and groups with an interest in or affected by the project
Significant checkpoints marking key achievements in the project
answer: Significant checkpoints marking key achievements in the project

question: 'Resource allocation' in project management
options: Tasks that cannot begin until other tasks are completed
The defined boundaries of what a project will and will not deliver
Assigning people, equipment and materials to project tasks
Planning, monitoring and controlling project costs
answer: Assigning people, equipment and materials to project tasks

question: The concept of 'project budget management'
options: Planning, monitoring and controlling project costs
Project tasks, durations and their timeline in a bar chart format
Significant checkpoints marking key achievements in the project
Assigning people, equipment and materials to project tasks
answer: Planning, monitoring and controlling project costs

question: 'Change control procedures'
options: Planning, monitoring and controlling project costs
Formal processes for approving and documenting changes to project scope
The defined boundaries of what a project will and will not deliver
All individuals and groups with an interest in or affected by the project
answer: Formal processes for approving and documenting changes to project scope

question: The concept of 'project closure'
options: Hierarchically decomposes project work into manageable components
Recording what went well and what could improve for future projects
The amount of time a task can be delayed without affecting the project end date
Completing all activities, delivering outputs and documenting lessons learned
answer: Completing all activities, delivering outputs and documenting lessons learned

question: 'Agile project management'
options: The amount of time a task can be delayed without affecting the project end date
Planning, monitoring and controlling project costs
Hierarchically decomposes project work into manageable components
Iterative approach delivering value in short cycles with continuous feedback
answer: Iterative approach delivering value in short cycles with continuous feedback

question: The concept of 'project dependencies'
options: Formal processes for approving and documenting changes to project scope
Tasks that cannot begin until other tasks are completed
Planning, monitoring and controlling project costs
The amount of time a task can be delayed without affecting the project end date
answer: Tasks that cannot begin until other tasks are completed

question: 'Float (slack) in project management'
options: Assigning people, equipment and materials to project tasks
Planning, monitoring and controlling project costs
The amount of time a task can be delayed without affecting the project end date
Project tasks, durations and their timeline in a bar chart format
answer: The amount of time a task can be delayed without affecting the project end date

question: The concept of 'lessons learned' documentation
options: All individuals and groups with an interest in or affected by the project
Tasks that cannot begin until other tasks are completed
Recording what went well and what could improve for future projects
Completing all activities, delivering outputs and documenting lessons learned
answer: Recording what went well and what could improve for future projects
